title: probabilistic-alert-smoothing-eval-gate: add stochastic CI gate to reduce noisy prompt-regression alerts
preview: Motivation: CI has been producing a high volume of transient prompt-regression alerts for low-volume cohorts. These noisy failures increase on-call toil and delay prioritization of true regressions. This change introduces a probabilistic/stochastic gating layer that smooths alerts by estimating a posterior over the ...
text: Motivation: CI has been producing a high volume of transient prompt-regression alerts for low-volume cohorts. These noisy failures increase on-call toil and delay prioritization of true regressions. This change introduces a probabilistic/stochastic gating layer that smooths alerts by estimating a posterior over the cohort regression rate and only opening an incident when the posterior crosses a configurable credibility threshold. Design: - New CI hook `prob-gate` computes a Beta posterior over passed/failed sample counts per cohort, with configurable prior (alpha/beta) and lookback window. - Gate emits three outcomes: allow-fail (treat as transient), gated-fail (record but do not page), and open-incident (page and block merge). - A lightweight failover path triggers a full evaluation run for cohorts that are near-threshold to reduce false positives. - Integrates with existing eval-har...

title: add difficulty-calibrator-and-heuristic-resampler-ci-hook
preview: Motivation: CI quality gates have been vulnerable to false positives/negatives when prompt sets shift subtly in difficulty (length, novelty vs. anchor set, generation entropy). This PR introduces a difficulty-calibrator component that scores prompts by a composite difficulty metric and a heuristic resampler used bot...
text: Motivation: CI quality gates have been vulnerable to false positives/negatives when prompt sets shift subtly in difficulty (length, novelty vs. anchor set, generation entropy). This PR introduces a difficulty-calibrator component that scores prompts by a composite difficulty metric and a heuristic resampler used both during local eval runs and as a CI hook to detect distributional shifts. What changed: 1) eval_harness/difficulty/calibrator.py: new module that computes a composite difficulty score per prompt (token-length-normalized surprisal proxy from a lightweight LM scoring pass, embedding distance to anchored prompt centroids, and response-model entropy estimate). 2) eval_harness/ci/hooks/difficulty_gate.py: CI hook that compares the baseline difficulty distribution (stored as a short histogram blob in CI artifacts) to the current run using a K-S style test plus a shift-aware regr...

title: Longtail SLO meter and annotation adapter
preview: Motivation: We have seen sporadic, low-volume prompt-set failures that escape per-run thresholds but aggregate into customer-visible issues over time (long tail). This PR implements a Longtail SLO Meter that maintains a decay-weighted time series of failure signals and a companion Annotation Adapter to attach contex...
text: Motivation: We have seen sporadic, low-volume prompt-set failures that escape per-run thresholds but aggregate into customer-visible issues over time (long tail). This PR implements a Longtail SLO Meter that maintains a decay-weighted time series of failure signals and a companion Annotation Adapter to attach contextual evidence (examples, model variant, env) to flagged prompt-sets. The goal is to surface gradual SLO drift earlier and enable actionable handoff to triage workflows without increasing noisy CI failures. Design summary: 1) longtail_meter module: computes an exponentially decayed aggregate for each prompt-cohort keyed by prompt_set_id + model_variant, supports configurable half-life and bucket lengths; 2) annotation_adapter: lightweight pluggable interface (sync+async) that writes structured evidence to eval metadata and an optional annotations store; 3) ci-integration: a ...

title: Contextual weighted sample allocator and gradual restrictor for stable eval gating
preview: Motivation: CI evals have been noisy for lower-volume prompt cohorts. Teams are getting frequent false positives when a small cohort flips. This PR implements a contextual weighted sample allocator that allocates probe budget across prompt sets using a combination of: (1) prompt-level importance (manually tagged or ...
text:
Motivation: CI evals have been noisy for lower-volume prompt cohorts. Teams are getting frequent false positives when a small cohort flips. This PR implements a contextual weighted sample allocator that allocates probe budget across prompt sets using a combination of: (1) prompt-level importance (manually tagged or derived from production hit rates), (2) recent drift score (rolling 7-day), and (3) semantic novelty (via embedding distance).

Design: The allocator produces proportional sample quotas per cohort and emits a per-run sampling plan. A complementary GradualRestrictor replaces the previous immediate hard-blocking CI hook: instead of a binary fail, we compute a severity score and apply a staged response (soft-fail annotations, delayed blocking windows, and adaptive replays). This reduces churn while preserving safety for true regressions.

What changed (high level):
- New alloc...

title: Introduce hierarchical fidelity oracle and non-blocking CI adapter
preview: Motivation: existing CI gates are brittle for prompt sets with heterogeneous lengths, multi-turn contexts and low-frequency anchors which lead to noisy false positives. This PR introduces a hierarchical fidelity oracle that fuses multiple evaluation signals (semantic similarity, role-aware turn consistency, token-le...
text: Motivation: existing CI gates are brittle for prompt sets with heterogeneous lengths, multi-turn contexts and low-frequency anchors which lead to noisy false positives. This PR introduces a hierarchical fidelity oracle that fuses multiple evaluation signals (semantic similarity, role-aware turn consistency, token-level saliency, and anchor stability) across temporal cohorts and prompt granularity, and exposes a non-blocking CI adapter that surfaces soft-fail quality regressions with prioritized remediation suggestions. Core behaviors: 1) compute per-prompt multi-signal fidelity vectors, 2) aggregate per-cohort with recency-aware weighting and uncertainty calibration, 3) emit composite fidelity score and explainability hints (top contributing tokens and failing turns), 4) provide a non-blocking CI decision path which fails only on high-confidence regressions and otherwise annotates PRs...

title: Introduce influence-matrix eval hook and rollback scorer
preview: Motivation: We've had recurring incidents where a small prompt tweak in a PR produced outsized regressions for a narrow but critical slice of prompt-sets. Existing CI smokechecks flag macro regressions but provide little signal about which prompts, tokens, or candidate edits are most influential. This PR adds an 'in...
text:
Motivation: We've had recurring incidents where a small prompt tweak in a PR produced outsized regressions for a narrow but critical slice of prompt-sets. Existing CI smokechecks flag macro regressions but provide little signal about which prompts, tokens, or candidate edits are most influential. This PR adds an 'influence matrix' eval hook that attributes per-prompt and per-perturbation influence on aggregate metrics, and a lightweight rollback scorer that suggests the smallest prompt change(s) that would return metrics to baseline.

What this does: Implements an eval hook that runs alongside existing regression checks. For each prompt-set it computes an influence score per prompt and per change vector using a randomized leave-k-out approximation (bounded sampling plus importance-weighting) and aggregates into an influence matrix (prompt x perturbation). The CI adapter consumes aggre...

title: Introduce contextual cohort synthesis gate with adaptive weighting
preview: Motivation: We frequently see spurious regression signals when prompt distributions shift subtly (new metadata, truncated contexts, or injected system instructions). This PR introduces a CI/managed-gate that synthesizes cohorts at evaluation time based on semantic context clusters and adaptively reweights alert sign...
text:
Motivation: We frequently see spurious regression signals when prompt distributions shift subtly (new metadata, truncated contexts, or injected system instructions). This PR introduces a CI/managed-gate that synthesizes cohorts at evaluation time based on semantic context clusters and adaptively reweights alert signals so short-term distribution shifts do not produce noisy failures.

What this PR does: 1) Adds a new gate type 'contextual_cohort_synthesis' that can be plugged into the CI eval pipeline; 2) Implements a lightweight vector-backed cohort builder that groups examples by a configurable semantic window (prompt+metadata embedding); 3) Computes weighted regression scores across synthesized cohorts with an attenuation factor tied to cohort volatility; 4) Exposes config knobs (min_cohort_size, volatility_smoothing, fallback_anchor) and a canary rollout policy; 5) Emits detailed t...

title: Add regression blame and diff-summary CI check for prompt-set evaluations
preview: Motivation: CI currently reports failing prompt-set metrics but gives limited context to quickly identify the source of a regression. This PR introduces a two-part CI hook: (1) regression-blame which correlates failing prompt cohorts to recent changes in prompt definitions, eval configs, and model pinning; (2) diff-...
text: Motivation: CI currently reports failing prompt-set metrics but gives limited context to quickly identify the source of a regression. This PR introduces a two-part CI hook: (1) regression-blame which correlates failing prompt cohorts to recent changes in prompt definitions, eval configs, and model pinning; (2) diff-summary which renders a compact human-readable summary of model-output changes for affected prompts. The goal is to reduce mean time-to-diagnose for eval regressions and to make gating decisions actionable. Implementation notes: regression-blame walks the prompt-set git history and the eval config commits in the last 30 days to produce candidate commit ranges; it then scores candidates by overlap with changed prompt IDs, changed tokenization metadata, and run-time seed/temperature differences. diff-summary runs a lightweight paired-output comparator that highlights token-le...

title: conditional eval fanout + weighted signal aggregator and CI gating
preview: Motivation: when a primary prompt-set check trips, we often need to run a set of targeted probes (e.g. hallucination prober, paraphrase-stability, token-saliency) to determine failure mode before blocking rollouts. This PR adds a conditional eval fanout layer and a weighted signal aggregator so CI can produce a sing...
text: Motivation: when a primary prompt-set check trips, we often need to run a set of targeted probes (e.g. hallucination prober, paraphrase-stability, token-saliency) to determine failure mode before blocking rollouts. This PR adds a conditional eval fanout layer and a weighted signal aggregator so CI can produce a single composite gating decision that reflects both severity and breadth of regressions. Design summary: - New runtime component eval_fanout orchestrates conditional parallel probes with a budget cap and backpressure to avoid blowing smoke-test windows. - WeightedSignalAggregator implements decay-weighted aggregation across cohorts and probes; weights are configurable per-linear-task and default to severity-first then coverage. - CiHook composite_slo_check integrates into existing eval CI: primary prompt-set runs as usual; on fail, fanout triggers configured probes; aggregator ...

title: Lightweight quality spot-check hooks for perf suite runs
preview: Summary: Perf regressions are easy to detect, but we’ve had a few near-misses where perf-focused changes accidentally altered decoding behavior (e.g., defaults drift, chat template mismatch, tool-call formatting toggled, or truncation due to max_tokens changes). This PR adds an optional, lightweight “quality spot-ch...
text:
Summary: Perf regressions are easy to detect, but we’ve had a few near-misses where perf-focused changes accidentally altered decoding behavior (e.g., defaults drift, chat template mismatch, tool-call formatting toggled, or truncation due to max_tokens changes). This PR adds an optional, lightweight “quality spot-check” hook to run at the end of a perf run, sampling a small prompt set and applying basic sanity checks. It is not intended to be a full quality gate; it is intended to catch obvious breakages early and provide artifacts for triage.

Motivation / context: As part of the Q1 2026 “Perf baseline & representative workload suite” work, we’re standardizing perf runs and integrating canaries/gating. Applied ML asked for minimal guardrails so that we don’t ship a perf win that is actually a decoding/config regression. This PR implements the minimal spot-check plan aligned with the ...

title: Introduce variant-weighted phased gate and explainable failure reporter
preview: Motivation: We need a gate that can phase-in stricter regression checks across multiple prompt-set variants and weight them by production traffic mix. Existing gates are either binary or treat variants equally, which causes noisy failures from low-impact variants and slow rollouts when a single high-volume variant c...
text:
Motivation: We need a gate that can phase-in stricter regression checks across multiple prompt-set variants and weight them by production traffic mix. Existing gates are either binary or treat variants equally, which causes noisy failures from low-impact variants and slow rollouts when a single high-volume variant changes. This PR implements a phased "variant-weighted" quality gate plus an explainable failure reporter that surfaces per-variant contribution and suggested mitigations.

Summary of changes: added a PhaseGate orchestration that accepts variant weights and stage thresholds, a scoring adapter that produces per-variant metrics (accuracy, token-loss, semantic-distance), and an explainability module that generates a short failure report with example prompts, model outputs, and a concise mitigation recommendation. The gate supports three phases (shadow, canary, enforce) and auto...

title: Introduce per-prompt temporal baseline probe, drift-aware CI gate, and lightweight counterfactual replayer
preview: Motivation: Some prompt-set regressions we observe are intermittent and tied to recent temporal shifts in training data or model weights. Existing cohort/anchor approaches flag regressions but often require manual baseline selection or suffer from noise when the underlying distribution evolves. This PR adds a Per-Pr...
text:
Motivation: Some prompt-set regressions we observe are intermittent and tied to recent temporal shifts in training data or model weights. Existing cohort/anchor approaches flag regressions but often require manual baseline selection or suffer from noise when the underlying distribution evolves. This PR adds a Per-Prompt Temporal Baseline (PTB) probe that maintains short- and long-window baselines per prompt, a drift-aware CI gate that reasons about recent baseline movement before failing a job, and a lightweight counterfactual replayer used to prioritize regressions for human review.

Design summary: The PTB probe (eval_harness.probes.ptb_probe) computes exponentially weighted aggregates (EWMA) of configured metrics per prompt across two windows: short (default 1d) and long (default 30d). The probe exposes three signals: absolute delta (short vs long), variance ratio, and a stability ...

title: Add trigger endpoint for Optimize quality gating (config diff + baseline)
preview: Context: Optimize needs a first-class way to run offline evals against a proposed serving config change (batching/caching/quantization profile) before allowing apply/rollout. This PR adds a new API endpoint and job plumbing in eval-harness to accept a config diff + baseline reference, execute the requested gate tier...
text: Context: Optimize needs a first-class way to run offline evals against a proposed serving config change (batching/caching/quantization profile) before allowing apply/rollout. This PR adds a new API endpoint and job plumbing in eval-harness to accept a config diff + baseline reference, execute the requested gate tier against selected prompt sets, and return structured metrics plus artifact pointers suitable for Console/Optimize consumption. Endpoint: POST /api/v1/gates/trigger (auth: service-to-service token; scoped to optimize-gating). Request (JSON): gate_tier ("smoke"|"full"), model_id, baseline (baseline_model_version + baseline_runtime_config_ref), proposed (runtime_config_diff, optional proposed_model_version), prompt_sets (ids), sample_count (optional; defaults per tier), run_metadata (route_id, account_id, region, commit_sha, idempotency_key). Validation: limits request payload...

title: Add token-attribution prober and rollback-suggestion CI adapter
preview: Motivation: We observed regressions that only manifest at the token level (e.g., a previously rare hallucinated sub-token pattern increasing frequency in certain prompt cohorts). Existing cohort-level SLOs catch change in aggregate metrics but not subtle token-attribution shifts. This PR introduces a lightweight tok...
text:
Motivation: We observed regressions that only manifest at the token level (e.g., a previously rare hallucinated sub-token pattern increasing frequency in certain prompt cohorts). Existing cohort-level SLOs catch change in aggregate metrics but not subtle token-attribution shifts. This PR introduces a lightweight token-attribution prober that runs as part of eval-harness smoke checks and a CI adapter that suggests minimally invasive rollback patches when attribution drift exceeds configured thresholds.

Summary: Adds a token-level attribution probe that integrates model attribution signals (attention-based saliency and logit-difference scoring) into the eval pipeline, produces per-prompt per-token drift heatmaps, and new CI logic that: 1) compares attribution fingerprints to historical anchors, 2) raises soft or hard failures depending on severity, and 3) emits rollback suggestions (pr...

title: Introduce proportional failure heatmap and context coverage CI hook
preview: Motivation: some regressions surface only in high-volume contexts or in narrow prompt cohorts; existing eval gates treat each failing example equally which underweights production impact. This PR introduces a proportional failure heatmap and a context-coverage metric that (1) weights failures by frequency and severi...
text: Motivation: some regressions surface only in high-volume contexts or in narrow prompt cohorts; existing eval gates treat each failing example equally which underweights production impact. This PR introduces a proportional failure heatmap and a context-coverage metric that (1) weights failures by frequency and severity, (2) surfaces top-left hotspots in a time-decayed heatmap, and (3) produces a single coverage score used by the CI hook. Design: we cluster prompts into contextual cohorts using the existing prompt fingerprinting + semantic shingle embedding. For each cohort we compute a weighted failure score = sum_{examples in cohort}(failure_weight * recency_decay * frequency_factor). Frequency comes from the sampled traffic histogram (or sample proxy during CI). Severity is derived from configured eval metrics (exact-match, BLEU drop, hallucination flag), mapped to a 1-5 weight. We r...

title: Implement prompt fingerprint ledger and replay-safe CI enforcer
preview: Motivation: Flaky eval failures at the prompt-set level continue to surface from subtle dataset composition drift (sampling, paraphrase swaps, and tokenization edge cases) rather than model regressions. This PR implements a compact on-disk ledger and a CI-side enforcer that (1) records stable fingerprints for prompt...
text:
Motivation: Flaky eval failures at the prompt-set level continue to surface from subtle dataset composition drift (sampling, paraphrase swaps, and tokenization edge cases) rather than model regressions. This PR implements a compact on-disk ledger and a CI-side enforcer that (1) records stable fingerprints for prompt-sets+seed+model-config, (2) compares new runs against the ledger to flag composition-driven variance, and (3) provides a replay-safe enforcement mode that permits human review and controlled backfills before a hard CI failure.

Design summary: The fingerprint is a deterministic, salted hash that combines prompt identifiers, normalized prompt text, canonical tokenization signature, run-seed, and a lightweight output sketch (sampled token-level checksums plus aggregated metrics). A new ledger store (local by default; support for S3/GCS) holds one canonical entry per evaluate...

title: Introduce anchor-driven failure replay with progressive quarantine throttler
preview: Motivation: Spikes of noisy eval failures (often transient or infra-related) are triggering rollbacks and incorrect regression signals. This PR introduces an anchor-driven failure replay system that replays a small, provenance-anchored sample of failing requests against the candidate and baseline in isolation, plus ...
text: Motivation: Spikes of noisy eval failures (often transient or infra-related) are triggering rollbacks and incorrect regression signals. This PR introduces an anchor-driven failure replay system that replays a small, provenance-anchored sample of failing requests against the candidate and baseline in isolation, plus a progressive quarantine throttler that limits the blast radius of cohorts that produce repeated non-deterministic failures. Key goals: reduce noisy CI failures, improve root-cause signal for real regressions, and keep reruns bounded and auditable. What this changes: 1) eval_harness/orchestrator/anchor_replay.py: new replay engine that captures request/response provenance and replays deterministically with KV-cache seeding; 2) ci/hooks/anchor_replay_hook.py: CI hook that marks failing eval jobs for anchored replay and triggers the replay job; 3) ci/configs/progressive_quara...

title: Introduce contextualized minibatch regression watchdog and suppression adapter
preview: Motivation: Flaky per-prompt regressions are noisy in CI when single minibatch failures trigger rollbacks or high-severity alerts. This PR adds a contextualized minibatch regression watchdog that computes short-horizon, context-aware regression signals and a suppression adapter that dampens alerts from transient min...
text:
Motivation: Flaky per-prompt regressions are noisy in CI when single minibatch failures trigger rollbacks or high-severity alerts. This PR adds a contextualized minibatch regression watchdog that computes short-horizon, context-aware regression signals and a suppression adapter that dampens alerts from transient minibatch noise. The goal is to reduce false positives in CI gating while preserving sensitivity to sustained quality regressions.

What this PR introduces: 1) src/watchdogs/contextual_minibatch_watchdog.py - new watchdog which calculates per-prompt-context rolling statistics (short window mean, variance, exponential smoothing) and emits a scored regression signal. 2) src/adapters/suppression_adapter.py - a pluggable adapter used in CI and alerting pipelines to apply hysteresis and suppression rules: confirmation windows, adaptive thresholds based on context prevalence, and a ...

title: Add ensemble-score-aggregator and CI regression hook for composite eval metrics
preview: Motivation: teams running multi-metric evals need a single, auditable signal for CI gating that reflects weighted regressions across prompt sets and metric families. This PR introduces an "ensemble score" aggregator that computes a weighted composite from a configurable set of metrics (e.g. BLEU, ROUGE-L, exact-matc...
text: Motivation: teams running multi-metric evals need a single, auditable signal for CI gating that reflects weighted regressions across prompt sets and metric families. This PR introduces an "ensemble score" aggregator that computes a weighted composite from a configurable set of metrics (e.g. BLEU, ROUGE-L, exact-match, embedding-sim) and a CI hook that fails builds when the ensemble score or any high-priority submetric regresses past configured thresholds. What changed: added aggregator implementation, config schema, CI integration, unit + integration tests, docs and migration notes. Checklist: [x] implementation, [x] schema & defaults, [x] unit tests, [x] integration test against sample prompt-sets, [x] CI job wired to GitHub Actions, [x] docs and example config in docs/evals. Related: Linear ENG-2141, ENG-2210; references: https://linear.app/redwood/issue/ENG-2141/ensemble-eval-signa...

title: introduce-historical-variance-detector-and-variance-gate-hook
preview: Motivation: We've seen intermittent metric oscillations across repeated eval runs (same prompt set, different seeds / sampling windows) that cause nondeterministic gate failures and noisy postdeploy regressions. This PR introduces a Historical Variance Detector (HVD) to detect when a prompt-set or cohort exhibits un...
text:
Motivation: We've seen intermittent metric oscillations across repeated eval runs (same prompt set, different seeds / sampling windows) that cause nondeterministic gate failures and noisy postdeploy regressions. This PR introduces a Historical Variance Detector (HVD) to detect when a prompt-set or cohort exhibits unstable score distributions over a sliding window of previous eval runs and optionally block merges.

What I changed (high level):
- Add a new module eval_harness/variance_detector.py that computes per-prompt and cohort-level variance, runs a two-sample KS test against the historical distribution, and produces a compact variance report.
- Add an opt-in GH Action workflow .github/workflows/variance-gate.yml that invokes the harness check with the new --variance-check flag on PRs targeting main and release/*.
- Extend the CLI: `harness check --suite <suite> --variance-check --...

title: Tag eval runs with model version + model-metadata schema hash
preview: Context: As part of the Model Metadata Schema v1 + automated changelog work, we need to be able to trace a customer-visible release note entry back to concrete eval evidence (and vice versa). Today the eval harness records the model identifier and some run params, but does not persist (a) the promoted model version ...
text: Context: As part of the Model Metadata Schema v1 + automated changelog work, we need to be able to trace a customer-visible release note entry back to concrete eval evidence (and vice versa). Today the eval harness records the model identifier and some run params, but does not persist (a) the promoted model version string used for serving (e.g., catalog version/variant) and (b) the model-metadata schema hash that the changelog generator references. That makes it hard to answer support questions like “which exact model version did this eval run validate?” and blocks automated linking in release notes. What this PR does: 1) Adds required fields to the eval run manifest/results payload: model_version (string) and model_metadata_schema_hash (sha256 hex). 2) Populates these fields from the run config when available, and falls back to resolving via the model registry client (best-effort) wh...

title: Introduce contextual ballot evaluator and safety-snapshot regression gate
preview: Motivation: CI-driven functional regressions occasionally pass simple metric thresholds but still cause low-probability failure modes (safety, hallucination) to regress. This PR adds two complementary CI primitives: a Contextual Ballot Evaluator (CBE) that runs configurable re-samples of model responses and decides ...
text:
Motivation: CI-driven functional regressions occasionally pass simple metric thresholds but still cause low-probability failure modes (safety, hallucination) to regress. This PR adds two complementary CI primitives: a Contextual Ballot Evaluator (CBE) that runs configurable re-samples of model responses and decides pass/fail by consensus ballots weighted by semantic similarity and prompt cohort importance; and a Safety Snapshot Gate that captures classifier score distributions at merge time and raises soft-fail alerts when tail mass shifts beyond tolerance.

What this change does: Adds a ballot aggregation engine with pluggable weighting (semantic similarity, precedent weight, and token-length normalization), a CI hook that executes N reruns in parallel with staggered seeds and computes vote distributions, and a safety snapshot artifact that is stored alongside CI artifacts for histor...

title: Introduce staggered probing schedule and failure-decay gate
preview: Summary: Adds a time-staggered probing scheduler and a failure-decay gate to the eval CI pipeline. The staggered prober spreads probe execution across configurable windows to avoid thundering on sensitive model endpoints and to produce smoother rolling signals. The failure-decay gate introduces an exponentially deca...
text:
Summary: Adds a time-staggered probing scheduler and a failure-decay gate to the eval CI pipeline. The staggered prober spreads probe execution across configurable windows to avoid thundering on sensitive model endpoints and to produce smoother rolling signals. The failure-decay gate introduces an exponentially decaying penalty for isolated failures so that transient noise does not immediately block rollouts.
Motivation: We observed several incidents where atomic spikes in flakiness (network blips, transient infra GC, tokenization flakiness) caused immediate hard failures in the regression gate, triggering noisy rollbacks. This change reduces false-positive blocks while preserving sensitivity to sustained regressions.
Design: - StaggeredProber: new scheduler that accepts a probe set and a window configuration (slot_count, jitter_seed, priority_buckets). It assigns probes to slots dete...

title: Add anchorwise fidelity audit and CI explainability hook
preview: Motivation: Teams running long-lived prompt sets have asked for more granular, explainable regression signals tied to 'anchors' (representative examples) so that CI failures are actionable without manual triage. This PR introduces an anchorwise fidelity audit that computes per-anchor fidelity and saliency deltas, su...
text:
Motivation: Teams running long-lived prompt sets have asked for more granular, explainable regression signals tied to 'anchors' (representative examples) so that CI failures are actionable without manual triage. This PR introduces an anchorwise fidelity audit that computes per-anchor fidelity and saliency deltas, surfaces interpretable failure reasons (e.g., token shift, hallucinated span, idempotency drop), and wires a CI hook that annotates PRs with a compact explainability report.

What this changes: adds a lightweight audit runner (hooks/anchorwise_audit.py) that: (1) replays the prompt-set against a reference baseline and the candidate change, (2) computes per-anchor metrics (fidelity_score, token_saliency_delta, semantic_overlap_pct), (3) runs a stacked heuristic classifier to attribute probable failure cause, and (4) emits a normalized JSON+CSV summary consumed by the CI hook. ...

title: Tag eval outputs with experiment variant identifiers + publish compact score stream for Console
preview: Motivation: Route-level A/B testing in Console needs a quality signal per {route, experiment_id, variant_id}. Today, eval-harness produces per-run artifacts (full JSON + HTML reports) but those are not consistently attributable to a routing experiment variant, and they’re too heavy for Console ingestion. This PR (1)...
text:
Motivation: Route-level A/B testing in Console needs a quality signal per {route, experiment_id, variant_id}. Today, eval-harness produces per-run artifacts (full JSON + HTML reports) but those are not consistently attributable to a routing experiment variant, and they’re too heavy for Console ingestion. This PR (1) propagates experiment attribution fields into eval execution and output artifacts, and (2) publishes a compact score stream suitable for near-real-time aggregation and dashboarding.

What’s included: (A) Variant tagging: add optional experiment context to eval runs, including experiment_id, variant_id, route_id, and a stable stickiness_key_hash (when provided), and persist these tags into (i) run metadata.json, (ii) per-prompt result rows, and (iii) summary.json. (B) Compact score stream: emit one record per {run_id, prompt_set_id, scorer_name} with normalized score, raw s...

title: Route-level A/B evals for quantization profiles (toggle + comparative metrics)
preview:
## Summary
Adds first-class support in eval-harness for running route-level A/B evaluations of the Quantization Profile Library.

This PR introduces:
- A route settings toggler that can set `quantization_profile_ref` for a route (and restore it) to compare baseline vs quantized behavior.
- A/B runner wiring that kee...
text:
## Summary
Adds first-class support in eval-harness for running route-level A/B evaluations of the Quantization Profile Library.

This PR introduces:
- A route settings toggler that can set `quantization_profile_ref` for a route (and restore it) to compare baseline vs quantized behavior.
- A/B runner wiring that keeps prompts and sampling stable and attributes results to (a) requested profile ref and (b) observed applied profile (from response metadata/log annotations when available).
- Comparative metric aggregation and a single `gating_report.json` artifact with pass/fail and “why” fields that map to the quantization regression thresholds.

## Motivation
As part of the Quantization Profile Library rollout, we need deterministic, auditable route-level evaluation that can gate publication and canary enablement. Offline prompt-set evals are necessary but insufficient: route-level enabl...

title: Introduce randomized counterfactual probe and soft-fail CI adapter
preview: Adds a randomized counterfactual probe to the eval-harness and a soft-fail CI adapter that surfaces regressions without hard-blocking merges unless they exceed configurable statistical thresholds. Motivation: many regressions are brittle to minor prompt edits; counterfactual probing produces small, semantically pres...
text: Adds a randomized counterfactual probe to the eval-harness and a soft-fail CI adapter that surfaces regressions without hard-blocking merges unless they exceed configurable statistical thresholds. Motivation: many regressions are brittle to minor prompt edits; counterfactual probing produces small, semantically preserving edits and measures sensitivity. Design: probe generator implements configurable perturbation strategies (entity swap, tense shift, negation flip, punctuation perturbation) with deterministic seeding for reproducibility. The CI adapter runs a small batched probe set against the PR head, computes a bootstrap-based p-value and effect-size estimate, and returns either OK, WARN, or FAIL based on thresholds. WARN is marked as a soft-failure that appears prominently in PR checks and in the console, encouraging fixes without stopping fast-moving teams. Includes CLI flags --c...

title: introduce-adaptive-fidelity-curator-and-compact-failure-summarizer-ci-hook
preview: Motivation: Our existing CI smokechecks treat every prompt in a prompt-set equally which leads to noisy failures when a small subset of high-variance prompts trigger alerts. This PR introduces two coordinated pieces: (1) Adaptive Fidelity Curator: a CI hook that assigns per-prompt fidelity weights using an exponenti...
text: Motivation: Our existing CI smokechecks treat every prompt in a prompt-set equally which leads to noisy failures when a small subset of high-variance prompts trigger alerts. This PR introduces two coordinated pieces: (1) Adaptive Fidelity Curator: a CI hook that assigns per-prompt fidelity weights using an exponential-moving-average of recent evaluation scores and token-level stability signals, and exposes a soft-fail gating policy that scales alert severity by weighted SLO violations; (2) Compact Failure Summarizer: a post-failure analyzer that clusters failing examples by semantic/diff similarity, extracts exemplars, and produces a one-page failure summary with suggested roll-back scope (prompt IDs or prompt-cohort) for on-call triage. Changes in this PR are intended to reduce noisy alerts, prioritize high-impact regressions, and provide engineers with an immediate, digestible failu...

title: Add bounded-confidence replay CI hook and progressive quarantine
preview: Motivation: intermittent prompt-set regressions commonly triggered by sampling noise result in noisy CI failures and unnecessary quarantines. This change introduces a bounded-confidence replay CI hook that adaptively replays failing cohorts until a statistically sound confidence interval for the failure rate is obta...
text: Motivation: intermittent prompt-set regressions commonly triggered by sampling noise result in noisy CI failures and unnecessary quarantines. This change introduces a bounded-confidence replay CI hook that adaptively replays failing cohorts until a statistically sound confidence interval for the failure rate is obtained (Wilson score interval) or until a configured cap is reached. The hook also integrates a progressive-quarantine policy that transitions prompt cohorts through soft->hard quarantine states based on posterior confidence rather than raw failure counts. Design: ci/hooks/bounded_confidence_replay.py implements the replay loop, statistics and retry backoff; ci/policies/progressive_quarantine.yaml contains policy knobs (min_samples, max_replays, target_ci_width, soft_threshold, hard_threshold, decay). We expose a CLI toggle (--ci-bounded-replay) and a feature flag in CI confi...

title: Introduce stochastic-consensus eval oracle and CI bridge for instability-aware gating
preview: Motivation: We see intermittent flakiness in prompt-set regressions that traditional deterministic smoke checks miss. This PR adds a stochastic consensus evaluator that runs multiple randomized perturbations/seed variations per prompt, computes a consensus distribution over binary/metric-level outcomes, and exposes ...
text: Motivation: We see intermittent flakiness in prompt-set regressions that traditional deterministic smoke checks miss. This PR adds a stochastic consensus evaluator that runs multiple randomized perturbations/seed variations per prompt, computes a consensus distribution over binary/metric-level outcomes, and exposes a probabilistic gate for CI. The goal is to reduce false positives (single-seed noise) while surfacing true instability and persistent regressions; it also provides quarantine suggestions for cohorts with high variance. What this implements: 1) eval_harness/oracles/stochastic_consensus.py - core evaluator implementing ensemble of perturbations, aggregation functions (consensus rate, trimmed mean, bootstrap CI), and an optional seed scheduler; 2) eval_harness/ci/consensus_ci_adapter.py - adapter that converts evaluator results into CI-friendly status with configurable thresh...

title: Add multi-turn coherence smokehook and turnwise replay adapter
preview: Motivation: A growing class of regressions only surface in multi-turn scenarios (turn-to-turn contradiction, context leakage, or degeneration across turns). Existing single-turn smokechecks miss these. This PR adds a multi-turn coherence smokehook and a turnwise replay adapter to detect and gate regressions that man...
text:
Motivation: A growing class of regressions only surface in multi-turn scenarios (turn-to-turn contradiction, context leakage, or degeneration across turns). Existing single-turn smokechecks miss these. This PR adds a multi-turn coherence smokehook and a turnwise replay adapter to detect and gate regressions that manifest across conversational turns.

What this PR introduces: 1) A coherence metric that combines (a) cross-turn semantic consistency (embedding-based similarity between prior assistant turns and subsequent responses) and (b) next-turn prediction stability (model likelihoods / token-level divergence when conditioned on different plausible prior paraphrases). 2) A smokehook that runs a compact multi-turn golden set on every canary and nightly CI run; the hook returns softfail/quarantine when drift exceeds thresholds. 3) A turnwise replay adapter which can: (i) expand failing ...

title: Add sparse error annotator and CI autorepair hook for flaky prompt-sets
preview: Summary: This PR introduces a lightweight 'sparse error annotator' and an accompanying CI autorepair hook aimed at reducing noisy, low-density failures in prompt-set regression checks. The annotator detects when a regression signal is concentrated in very few prompts or is likely caused by brittle input formatting; ...
text: Summary: This PR introduces a lightweight 'sparse error annotator' and an accompanying CI autorepair hook aimed at reducing noisy, low-density failures in prompt-set regression checks. The annotator detects when a regression signal is concentrated in very few prompts or is likely caused by brittle input formatting; the autorepair hook runs a set of deterministic canonicalizers on flagged examples and re-executes the failing tests in CI. If the example passes post-repair the check is quarantined as a probable flake instead of failing the entire job. Motivation: We saw recurring alerts in canary / smoke evals where 1-2 prompts per prompt-set caused CI failures that were later determined to be formatting or tokenization edge-cases. These false positives interrupt rollouts and cause unnecessary investigations. This change reduces noisiness while preserving sensitivity to real regressions.

title: Introduce soft-label probing and calibrated-expectation CI hook
preview: Context and motivation: Some regressions manifest as shifts in the model's probability mass over semantically equivalent answers rather than hard label changes. Existing binary pass/fail gates can be too brittle (false positives) or miss subtle quality drift. This PR adds a 'soft-label prober' and a calibrated-expec...
text:
Context and motivation: Some regressions manifest as shifts in the model's probability mass over semantically equivalent answers rather than hard label changes. Existing binary pass/fail gates can be too brittle (false positives) or miss subtle quality drift. This PR adds a 'soft-label prober' and a calibrated-expectation CI hook that (a) probes prompt cohorts with synthetic soft labels (probability distributions over acceptable outputs), (b) compares aggregate likelihood mass assigned to expected semantic clusters, and (c) uses a noise-aware historical model to set dynamic tolerances so teams get fewer spurious CI failures while retaining sensitivity to true regressions.

Goals: 1) detect semantic drift that doesn't appear as single-answer mismatches; 2) reduce noisy CI failures by modeling historical variance per-cohort; 3) provide clear remediation artifacts (example worst-case pro...

title: Add differential prioritizer + regression demux CI hook for stratified smoke sampling
preview: Context and motivation: We continue to see noisy regression alerts from broad smoke-suites where rare but high-variance prompts swamp triage cycles. This PR introduces a sampling prioritizer that differentially weights prompts by historical variance, recent degradation signal, and business impact metadata, and a CI ...
text:
Context and motivation: We continue to see noisy regression alerts from broad smoke-suites where rare but high-variance prompts swamp triage cycles. This PR introduces a sampling prioritizer that differentially weights prompts by historical variance, recent degradation signal, and business impact metadata, and a CI hook (regression-demux) that demultiplexes failing prompts into per-model/cohort lanes for targeted replays and quarantines. The goal is to reduce alert fatigue while preserving early detection for high-impact regressions.

What this PR does (high level): implements eval_harness.samplers.differential_prioritizer, extends sample metadata to carry priority scores and buckets, adds a ci/hooks/regression_demux adapter that: (1) inspects CI eval failures, (2) enriches failures with priority and cohort context, (3) routes items to dedicated replay queues and labeling workflows, a...

title: Introduce severity-calibrated drift probe and prioritized failover CI hook
preview: Motivation: Prompt-set regressions are noisy — some regressions are low-impact (minor phrasing differences) while others are high-severity (safety or major quality drop). Existing CI gates treat all failures equally, producing noisy alerts and hard-to-interpret failures. This PR introduces a severity-calibrated prob...
text:
Motivation: Prompt-set regressions are noisy — some regressions are low-impact (minor phrasing differences) while others are high-severity (safety or major quality drop). Existing CI gates treat all failures equally, producing noisy alerts and hard-to-interpret failures. This PR introduces a severity-calibrated probe that fuses multiple eval metrics into a single ordinal severity score, and wires a prioritized failover CI hook that uses that score to decide whether to soft-fail, block, or recommend an automatic failover to a designated fallback model or route.

What this changes (high level): commit 1 adds the probe implementation and a lightweight aggregator that accepts multiple metric adapters (accuracy delta, semantic-similarity drift, latency-penalty, toxicity spike). commit 2 implements a CI hook integration that runs the probe as part of nightly and pre-merge smoke checks and e...

title: add-perspective-weighted-eval-gate-and-selective-replay-hook
preview: Motivation: standard aggregate eval gates (mean / median-based) can mask systematic, perspective-driven regressions where a model drifts at the cohort-level for a particular annotator perspective (e.g. cultural framing, domain expertise). This PR introduces a lightweight perspective-weighted scoring function and a C...
text:
Motivation: standard aggregate eval gates (mean / median-based) can mask systematic, perspective-driven regressions where a model drifts at the cohort-level for a particular annotator perspective (e.g. cultural framing, domain expertise). This PR introduces a lightweight perspective-weighted scoring function and a CI gate that uses stratified bootstrapped confidence intervals across perspective clusters to surface regressions that would otherwise be attenuated by averaging. It also adds a selective-replay hook that schedules targeted re-evals for failing perspectives with deterministic sampling for reproducibility.

What this change contains: perspective clustering (unsupervised seed + optional annotation tag), per-perspective metric aggregation, perspective-weighted composite score, CI gate implementation wired into the existing CI hooks, and a selective replay orchestration that bac...
